channel.title: Money Intel Daily
channel.description: One money idea every day — with real numbers, not just headlines.

item.title: Gold vs S&P 500 in 2026: The $416,800 Difference (Real Math)
item.description: Gold just hit record highs in 2026 and everyone's calling it the ultimate 'safe haven.' But is it actually building wealth — or quietly costing you six figures? In this video, I run the REAL numbers: $10,000 in gold vs $10,000 in the S&P 500 over 20 years, plus what happens if you're 28 with $10K and $200/month to invest for the next 30 years.

item.title: Investing at 26 vs 31: The $572,000 Difference Explained
item.description: Waiting just 5 years to start investing in index funds could cost you $572,000 — and today I'm showing you the exact math, not a vague estimate.

item.title: AI Money Advice: The $422,000 Mistake Nobody Shows You
item.description: A new study covered by CNBC this week found that AI chatbots regularly give outdated, incomplete, or flat-out wrong personal finance advice — and most people never double-check it.

item.title: Stock Picking vs Index Funds: The $606,000 Difference (2026)
item.description: Gen Z is investing earlier than any generation in history — but new data suggests most are doing it in a way that quietly costs them six figures.

item.title: Dave Ramsey vs Index Funds: The $821,000 Fee Difference
item.description: Dave Ramsey vs Caleb Hammer is trending — but the real story isn't the drama, it's the math.

item.title: Investing at 30 vs 40: The $626,000 Difference (2026)
item.description: New Investopedia data just dropped on the average investment portfolio for people in their 30s — and the gap between the average ($68,000) and the median ($22,000) tells the whole story about who's actually building wealth.

item.title: If You Invested $100/Month in 2026: The $632,000 Result
item.description: A new CNBC report just revealed something huge: more low- and middle-income Americans are investing than ever before — and the math behind why is staggering.

item.title: 1% Advisor Fee: The $821,000 Cost Nobody Shows You
S&P 500 vs Real Estate: The $422,000 Gap Nobody Shows You
item.description: A new USA Today report confirms it: America's favorite investment is real estate. But is your favorite investment actually your best one? In this video, we

item.title: Lump Sum vs Monthly Investing: The $142K Difference (Real Math)
item.description: Dave Ramsey just sparked a debate: should you invest $350K all at once or spread it over months? We're running the actual numbers on both strategies using

item.title: Savings Account vs Inflation: The $12,400 Loss You're Having Right Now
item.description: Your savings account is quietly destroying your wealth. Here's the exact math: if you have $25,000 sitting in a 4.5% savings account while inflation runs a

item.title: Savings Account vs Inflation: The $8,400 Loss Nobody Mentions
item.description: Your savings account is costing you $700/year in lost purchasing power. Here's the exact math on why traditional savings fail Gen Z—and the ONE move that f
